reasoning: The market price on October 30, 2025 was 4%. As of the knowledge cutoff (November 9, 2025), there are only 52 days remaining in the year. The base rate for WHO declaring a pandemic in any given year is extremely low (perhaps 1-2 times per century). There are ongoing disease outbreaks (such as Mpox Clade I and H5N1 avian influenza), but historically the WHO rarely uses the term 'pandemic' – instead typically using Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC). Given the short time horizon remaining in 2025 and the lack of a rapidly escalating global respiratory event as of late October, the probability of a declaration before December 31 is extremely low. I am setting the probability to the minimum allowed or close to it (0.02).
